phases.name: Year 1
phases.subtitle: Explore
phases.description: Foundation in biological and chemical sciences with introduction to nutrition science career pathways.

milestones.label: Explore nutrition science careers
milestones.description: Survey of nutrition science fundamentals including macronutrients, micronutrients, digestion, and metabolism. Students explore career pathways in clinical dietetics, community nutrition, sports nutrition, and public health.

milestones.label: Build chemistry foundations
milestones.courseRef: CHEM 106/116 — General Chemistry I with Lab
milestones.description: Foundational chemistry for understanding biochemical reactions, nutrient metabolism, and pharmacokinetics. Laboratory skills essential for clinical and research nutrition.

milestones.label: Study biological science fundamentals
milestones.description: Cell biology, genetics, and human physiology foundations that underpin nutritional biochemistry and clinical nutrition coursework.

milestones.label: Understand behavioral foundations
milestones.description: Introduction to human behavior, motivation, and behavioral change models. Essential for nutrition counseling, eating disorder awareness, and community nutrition program design.

phases.name: Year 2
phases.subtitle: Engage
phases.description: Intermediate nutrition science, organic chemistry, and community engagement build the scientific core for ACEND competencies.

milestones.label: Master intermediate nutrition science
milestones.description: In-depth study of nutrient metabolism, energy balance, and nutritional requirements across the lifespan. Students analyze dietary patterns and evaluate evidence-based dietary guidelines.

milestones.label: Study lifespan nutrition needs
milestones.description: Nutritional requirements during pregnancy, lactation, infancy, childhood, adolescence, and aging. Students assess nutritional needs of vulnerable populations and develop age-appropriate dietary recommendations.

milestones.label: Complete organic chemistry
milestones.description: Organic molecular structure, functional groups, and reaction mechanisms. Essential for understanding vitamin chemistry, drug-nutrient interactions, and metabolic pathway biochemistry.

milestones.label: Develop counseling communication skills
milestones.description: Professional communication skills for nutrition education, client counseling, and community health presentations. Students practice motivational interviewing techniques relevant to dietary behavior change.

phases.name: Year 3
phases.subtitle: Develop
phases.description: Advanced clinical nutrition, assessment techniques, and community nutrition practice build professional-grade expertise for dietetics pathways.

milestones.label: Master nutritional assessment methods
milestones.description: Clinical assessment methods including anthropometric measurements, biochemical indicators, dietary recall analysis, and clinical signs of nutrient deficiency. Students conduct comprehensive nutritional assessments on real clients.

milestones.label: Study medical nutrition therapy
milestones.description: Evidence-based nutrition interventions for chronic diseases including diabetes, cardiovascular disease, obesity, and renal disease. Students develop medical nutrition therapy care plans and evaluate clinical outcomes.

milestones.label: Complete advanced nutrition biochemistry
milestones.description: In-depth biochemical mechanisms of nutrient metabolism, gene-nutrient interactions, and molecular nutrition. Prepares students for graduate research and the advanced science component of the RDN exam.

milestones.label: Design community nutrition programs
milestones.description: Community-based nutrition assessment, program planning, implementation, and evaluation. Students partner with local agencies to develop food security initiatives and nutrition education programs for underserved populations in Guilford County.

phases.name: Year 4
phases.subtitle: Launch
phases.description: Clinical practicum, capstone research, and professional placement finalize ACEND competencies and dietetics pathway readiness.

milestones.label: Complete clinical nutrition practicum
milestones.description: Supervised clinical experience in hospital, outpatient, or long-term care settings. Students apply medical nutrition therapy, conduct patient education, and work alongside registered dietitians in clinical rotations.

milestones.label: Complete nutrition research capstone
milestones.description: Independent research project investigating a nutrition science question. Students design studies, collect data, and present findings in a professional conference format. Demonstrates research competency for ACEND requirements.

milestones.label: Complete dietetics internship placement
milestones.description: Supervised internship at a clinical facility, WIC office, school nutrition program, or corporate wellness center. Students build supervised practice hours and professional networks for dietetic internship applications.

milestones.label: Complete specialization elective
milestones.description: Specialized coursework in sports nutrition, international nutrition, food policy, or nutritional genomics based on career focus area and ACEND competency requirements.
